source_pr.title: stmtsummary: fix data race in test
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: mornyx <mornyx.z@gmail.com>

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #41275 

### What is changed and how it works?

Fix data race in `stmtsummary_test.go`.

### Check List

Tests

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Race detector reports a data race in statement summary window rotation/persist logic exercised by TestStmtSummary (util/stmtsummary/v2/stmtsummary_test.go).
failure_signature: DATA RACE in StmtSummary.rotateLoop / async persist triggered by TestStmtSummary (util/stmtsummary/v2/stmtsummary_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by a concurrency/data-race issue in the statement summary rotation path: rotation triggers an asynchronous persist goroutine while the test also manipulates timing/timeNow and storage waiting, leading to concurrent access to shared state. The fix factors the rotation logic into a dedicated `rotate(now)` helper and updates the unit test to call `rotate()` directly (instead of relying on time mocking + a waitable mock storage), making the test deterministic and removing the racy async interaction.
fix_pattern: Refactor rotation into a helper and make the test call it directly to avoid racing with async persist goroutines.

evidence: evidence: stmtsummary: fix data race in test
